name: Lake Pontchartrain
description:
Lake Pontchartrain is a massive brackish estuary bordering New Orleans, spanning 630 square miles. Despite its urban setting, the lake supports an outstanding saltwater fishery with speckled trout and redfish as the primary targets.

Speckled trout fishing is productive around the causeway bridge pilings, which create over 24 miles of fish-holding structure. Redfish inhabit the shallow marshes and shell reefs around the lake's edges. The Rigolets and Chef Menteur Pass connect the lake to the Gulf, providing migration corridors for saltwater species. Multiple launches around the lake provide convenient access from both the north and south shores.

name: Toledo Bend Reservoir
description:
Toledo Bend Reservoir on the Louisiana-Texas border is one of the premier bass fishing lakes in the nation, consistently ranked among the top bass fisheries in the country. The 185,000-acre reservoir was built on the Sabine River.

Largemouth bass exceeding 13 pounds are caught every year, and the lake has hosted numerous Bassmaster and FLW tournaments. The extensive hydrilla beds and standing timber in the lake's numerous arms create outstanding bass habitat. Crappie fishing around submerged timber is exceptional during winter and spring. Multiple ramps and marinas serve both the Louisiana and Texas sides.

name: Grand Isle
description:
Grand Isle is Louisiana's only inhabited barrier island and the state's premier saltwater fishing destination. Its location at the mouth of Barataria Bay provides access to both inshore marsh fishing and offshore Gulf of Mexico waters.

Inshore fishing for redfish and speckled trout is productive year-round in the surrounding marshes and bays. Offshore, the Gulf's oil platforms create artificial reef habitat that holds red snapper, yellowfin tuna, and cobia. The annual Grand Isle International Tarpon Rodeo is the oldest fishing tournament in the US. The state park and marina provide launch access.

name: Calcasieu Lake
description:
Calcasieu Lake, known locally as Big Lake, is one of Louisiana's most productive saltwater estuaries for trophy speckled trout. The shallow, brackish lake in southwestern Louisiana regularly produces speckled trout exceeding 7 pounds.

The lake gained national attention for producing numerous speckled trout over 9 pounds, rivaling the best trout fisheries anywhere on the Gulf Coast. Redfish are equally abundant, with bull reds exceeding 40 pounds common in fall. The shallow depth makes it ideal for wade fishing and poling skiffs. Several public ramps provide access from the Lake Charles area.

name: Atchafalaya Basin
description:
The Atchafalaya Basin is the largest river swamp in the United States, a vast network of bayous, lakes, and cypress swamps spanning nearly a million acres in south-central Louisiana. The basin provides unmatched freshwater fishing in a wild, primeval setting.

Largemouth bass fishing is exceptional in the flooded timber and cypress-lined bayous, with fish reaching double digits. Crappie (sac-a-lait) fishing is a deeply rooted cultural tradition, with winter and spring producing incredible catches. Crawfishing supplements the rod-and-reel fishing. Multiple launches along Interstate 10 and at Henderson provide access into this remarkable ecosystem.

name: False River
description:
False River is an oxbow lake formed when the Mississippi River changed course centuries ago, leaving behind a crescent-shaped lake in Pointe Coupee Parish. The lake is one of the most picturesque and productive bass fisheries in Louisiana.

Largemouth bass fishing is the primary attraction, with the lake's mix of docks, vegetation, and old river structure providing excellent habitat. Crappie fishing is outstanding during winter, with night fishing under lights being particularly productive. A state public launch and several private launches provide access to this relatively compact but highly productive lake.

name: Cross Lake
description:
Cross Lake is Shreveport's primary water supply and a popular urban fishery in northwest Louisiana. The cypress-lined lake provides excellent bass and crappie fishing within minutes of the city.

Largemouth bass thrive in the standing cypress timber and lily pad fields, with fish exceeding 8 pounds caught regularly. Spring crappie fishing around the bridge pilings and cypress knees is a local tradition. The lake's accessibility from Shreveport makes it one of the most-fished lakes in the region. Multiple city and parish launches provide access.

name: Lake D'Arbonne
description:
Lake D'Arbonne is a large, cypress-filled lake in north Louisiana known for excellent bass and crappie fishing in a scenic woodland setting. The shallow, timber-rich lake provides classic Louisiana swamp fishing.

Largemouth bass inhabit the flooded timber and cypress stands throughout the lake, with spring and fall producing the best catches. Crappie fishing is outstanding during winter, when fish school around submerged structure and can be caught in large numbers. The lake's character changes with water level, creating shifting patterns that keep the fishing interesting. State park and public ramps provide access.
